title: Hadoop MapReduce Library Architecture
description: The 'lib' directory in Hadoop MapReduce architecture encapsulates various Java libraries that extend the functionality of MapReduce jobs. The 'aggregate' subdirectory provides value aggregation classes, enabling operations like summing, finding max/min values, and counting unique items. The 'db' subdirectory facilitates database integration, allowing MapReduce jobs to interact with SQL databases. The 'input' directory includes classes for input handling and error management, ensuring robust input processing. The 'map' directory contains specialized mappers, such as RegexMapper, for specific data extraction tasks. Lastly, the 'output' directory offers classes for output tracking, like FileOutputFormatCounter, aiding in job performance analysis. This hierarchical structure exemplifies modular and reusable design principles, adhering to patterns like Adapter, Strategy, Observer, and Composite.
